name: Eddie Beal
abstract: Eddie Beal (June 13, 1910, Redlands, California – December 15, 1984, Los Angeles) was an American jazz pianist. He was the brother of Charlie Beal.

about_quotes.speaker: Abbey Lincoln
about_quotes.evidence: I was offered a job at the Moulin Rouge through Eddie Beal, who was my coach.
about_quotes.doc_title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: And here's another thing of my Sextet, with Marshall Royal, Maxwell Davis, Floyd Turner, Eddie Beal, and with Chico Hamilton on drums and Albert Calderone on timbales.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: He wanted me to join a band with a Slim and Slam, and Lee and Lester Young, Bumps Meyers, and Jake Porter and Eddie Beal

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, Red Callender and Eddie Beal. Ulysses Banks, Oscar Bradley, Lloyd Reese and Leo Trammel. As you'll notice, the instrumentation is pretty much the same type of instrumentation that they used with the Kirby group.

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I associate it with Eddie Beal's house; everybody used to go to Eddie Beal's house and there used to be music in Eddie Beal's living room all the time.

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: When he didn't play 'Rhapsody in Blue' we had Eddie Beal. Eddie Beal was on the piano. He was our original piano.
